$comment: Pack definitions — the source of truth for what ships beyond the core bundle.
Adding a pack: add an entry here, add test/fixtures/packs/<id>.tex that uses it, then
run `pnpm bundle:packs`. The fixture is not optional: presence in a manifest is not
evidence a package works, which is exactly how siunitx once shipped listed-but-broken.
See PACKS.md for the design and the invariants.

packs.label: Writing & page layout
packs.description: Headers, footers, section styling, line spacing, contents lists, nomenclature and title pages. What a thesis front matter needs.

packs.label: Figures & placement
packs.description: Figure positioning, text wrapping, subfigures, resizing, landscape pages and PDF inclusion.

packs.label: Coloured boxes
packs.description: tcolorbox — framed, coloured and breakable boxes for notes and theorems.

packs.label: Annotation & review
packs.description: Margin notes, highlighting, strikethrough and cancelled maths.

packs.label: Science notation
packs.description: Physics vector notation and chemical formulae.

packs.label: Advanced tables
packs.description: Row shading, matrix-style and modern table layouts beyond the core table support.

packs.label: Bibliographies & citations
packs.description: BibTeX bibliographies end to end: the standard .bst styles, natbib author-year citations, csquotes quoting, and biblatex. biblatex needs \usepackage[backend=bibtex]{biblatex} — Biber is a Perl program and cannot run in the browser.

packs.label: Drafting helpers
packs.description: Placeholder text and flexible list customisation for drafts.

packs.label: Algorithm pseudocode
packs.description: Floating algorithm environments and the algorithmicx pseudocode syntax (\State, \While, \Procedure). Core ships algorithm2e, which is a different, incompatible syntax.

packs.label: Journal & conference classes
packs.description: IEEE and Elsevier submission classes. Many templates carry their own copy; these are for the ones that assume the class is installed.

packs.label: Beamer themes
packs.description: Every built-in beamer theme (Madrid, Berlin, Warsaw, …) plus their colour, font, inner and outer variants. Core ships only the default theme.

packs.label: Latin Modern fonts (T1)
packs.description: Type1 outlines for the default font family, needed to embed glyphs under \usepackage[T1]{fontenc}. Core carries the definitions and metrics; these are the outlines.
